design_philosophy.core_loop: Design and build roller coasters and themed rides, manage park operations (finance, guest satisfaction, maintenance), iterate on park layout to maximize revenue and guest happiness
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing creative expression (custom coaster design) with operational constraints (budget, guest safety ratings, maintenance costs)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become an amusement park mogul: design iconic attractions, manage a living ecosystem of guests with individual needs, build a thriving entertainment empire
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Open-ended management with no single 'correct' path; emphasizes player creativity in ride design and park layout rather than narrative-driven goals. Guests are simulated as individuals with distinct preferences, creating emergent gameplay

difficulty.checkpointing: Game state can be saved at any time; no failure states in sandbox mode
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Scenario-based challenges with preset objectives (financial targets, guest count, park rating) offer varying difficulty; sandbox mode removes all constraints allowing relaxed creative play

progression.upgrade_system: Unlock new ride types, scenery objects, and building materials as park develops; guest feedback and park rating unlock advanced options
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression tied to park financial performance, guest satisfaction metrics, and scenario completion rather than linear level advancement
replayability_hooks: Scenario challenges with different starting conditions and objectives
Sandbox mode for unlimited creative iteration
Diverse terrain and climate settings affect strategy
Guest behavioral simulation creates unique situations per playthrough

world_building_techniques: Themed park areas with cohesive aesthetics
Guest names and individual behavior patterns create emergent narratives
Scenario objectives (e.g., 'save a failing park') provide light narrative framing
Park progression visually demonstrates economic and operational success

faction_indicators: Park theme choice reflects player identity (family-friendly vs. thrill-seeker focused)
Ride selection and naming conventions express player aesthetic preferences
Financial strategy (profitable vs. guest-satisfaction focused) signals values
danger_signals: Neglected maintenance leading to guest injuries signals carelessness or experimentation gone wrong
Extreme pricing or poor service indicates exploitative management style
Dangerous coaster designs (high fatality rates) may indicate recklessness or dark humor
environmental_storytelling: Park layout and interconnected zones show player's spatial planning philosophy
Visual progression from ramshackle start to polished empire demonstrates mastery
Themed areas reveal player interest in specific attraction types or historical periods
Presence/absence of basic amenities (bathrooms, food, entertainment) shows management priorities

sources_used: wikipedia: RollerCoaster Tycoon series overview and gameplay description
